content:
𝗣𝗢𝗟𝗔𝗥𝗜𝗦 | To provide a self-hosted, personal music streaming service accessible from any device.  🔥 Super Hot!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a self-hosted, personal music streaming service accessible from any device.
📅 Created: Aug 2016 • Updated: Jul 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Self-hosted music streaming server
✅ Support for large music collections (100,000+ songs)
✅ Beautiful and customizable user interface with dark mode
✅ Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, Linux, BSD, Docker)
✅ Support for various audio file formats (flac, mp3, ogg, opus, etc.)
✅ Browse music by album, artist, genre, or file tree
✅ Multi-value metadata support
✅ Powerful search functionality
✅ Multi-user support with individual playlists
✅ Companion Android and iOS apps


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 127 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 26 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Polaris is a self-hosted, open-source music streaming server designed for exceptional performance and ease of use. It allows users to access their entire music collection from any device, supporting large libraries and offering a beautiful, customizable user interface.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/agersant/polaris

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

👉 GitHub Open Source: https://t.me/GitHub_Open_Source

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗗𝗟𝗠𝗔𝗡 | Accelerating and managing file downloads across multiple platforms with enhanced reliability and control.  🔥 Super Hot!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Accelerating and managing file downloads across multiple platforms with enhanced reliability and control.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Multi-segment parallel downloads (1–32 segments per file)
✅ Crash-safe persistence with SQLite
✅ Pause and resume anytime
✅ Auto-resume after crashes or restarts
✅ Scheduling & Automation (start/stop times, post-download actions)
✅ Queue management with priorities
✅ Categories with custom download folders
✅ Site login credentials saving and auto-apply
✅ CLI tool for scripts and automation
✅ Browser extensions


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 187 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 18 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

DLMan is a free, open-source download manager for Windows, macOS, and Linux, built with Rust and Tauri for speed and reliability. It offers advanced features like multi-segment downloads, crash-safe persistence with SQLite, scheduling, and organization tools.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/novincode/dlman

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗣𝗗𝗙_𝗦𝗛𝗜𝗘𝗟𝗗 | Detecting and preventing DoS and embedded JavaScript attacks within PDF files.  🔍 Worth a Look

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Tcl 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Detecting and preventing DoS and embedded JavaScript attacks within PDF files.
📅 Created: Sep 2023 • Updated: Oct 2025

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Automated PDF Monitoring
✅ Drag & Drop scanning
✅ Customizable Alerts (JS, infinite loops, zip bombs)
✅ Extensible plugin architecture
✅ User-Friendly Interface (CLI & GUI)
✅ Cross-Browser Defense (Chrome, Edge, Brave, PDF.js)


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐ 5 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

PDF_Shield is a Python-based security tool designed to detect and prevent Denial of Service (DoS) attacks and embedded JavaScript threats within PDF files. It offers automated monitoring, customizable alerts, and a user-friendly interface to safeguard against malicious PDF content, particularly targeting PDF engines in browsers.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/whiteSHADOW1234/PDF_Shield
